sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN ONE WORD OR A NUMBER.
Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.content: The police officer suggests neighbours give each other their {{BLANK}}
Neighbours should discuss what to do if there’s any kind of {{BLANK}}
It’s a good idea to leave on the {{BLANK}}
Think carefully about where you put any {{BLANK}}
It’s a good idea to buy good-quality {{BLANK}}

options: A install more lighting
B have more police officers on patrol
C remove surrounding vegetation
D contact local police
E fix damage quickly
F change road design
G use security cameras

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C for Questions 21-26. Answer the questions below for Questions 27-30. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context: Presentation on the problems and potential of biofuels

questions.content: Mike suggests they begin their presentation by
options: A explaining what kind of harm is caused by fossil fuels.
B pointing out that biofuels were in use before fossil fuels.
C ensuring students know the difference between fossil fuels and biofuels.

questions.content: Karina doesn’t want to discuss the production of ethanol because
options: A other students will already be familiar with the process.
B there will not be time to cover more important information.
C they may not provide an accurate description.

questions.content: Which source of biofuel do the students agree is least environmentally friendly?
options: A sugar cane
B corn
C canola

questions.content: What is the main problem facing the development of the biofuel industry in the USA?
options: A inadequate infrastructure for transporting ethanol
B not enough farmers growing biofuel crops
C little government support of biofuel development

questions.content: Karina doubts that sugar cane production in Brazil will
options: A lead to the loss of wildlife habitats.
B create a large number of jobs in the biofuel sector.
C continue to provide enough energy for the country’s needs.

questions.content: Karina and Mike conclude that in order to increase the use of biofuels
options: A the price of fossil fuels must go up.
B more machinery must be adapted to use them.
C production methods must be more energy-efficient.

sections.instruction: Complete the summary below for Questions 31-34. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer. Choose TWO letters, A-E for Questions 35-40.

questions.content:
The ‘weak-tie’ theory: how friends-of-friends influence us
In 1973, Mark Granovetter claimed that the influence of ‘weak-ties’ can affect the behaviour of populations in the fields of information science, politics and {{BLANK}}. Although friends-of-friends may be unlike us, they have similar enough {{BLANK}} to have a beneficial effect on our lives. An example of this influence is when we hear about {{BLANK}}. Because information about them is provided by weak-ties. Since Granovetter proposed his theory, other studies have shown that weak-tie networks also benefit our {{BLANK}}.
Which does the speaker believe are TWO real benefits of online social networking?
options: A people can gain higher self-esteem
B people can access useful medical information
C people can form relationships more quickly
D people can improve academic performance
E people can be reliably informed about current affairs

questions.content: Which TWO problems related to online social networking will increase, according to the speaker?
options: A criminal activity
B poorer grades at school
C a decline in physical fitness
D less work done by employees
E loss of career prospects

questions.content: Which TWO claims are made by Robin Dunbar about social networking sites?
options: A They are not helpful for developing certain social skills.
B They cannot fully reveal a person’s real character.
C They are not a good starting point for building new relationships.
D They do not encourage people to widen their social circle.
E They will not retain their popularity with the young generation.
